statement: Nihlathak uses Corpse Explosion (CE) which deals 10-20% of the corpse's maximum life as Physical and Fire damage. This is the primary danger when farming him.
To enter the Secret Cow Level, transmute Wirt's Leg and a Tome of Town Portal in the Horadric Cube while in the Rogue Encampment (Act 1).
formula: Wirt's Leg + Tome of Town Portal = Red Portal

statement: Terror Zones are a feature introduced in Patch 2.5. In online games, one zone is terrorized per hour (rotates every hour). Terrorized monsters are boosted to your character level +2 (champion +4, unique +5), up to level 96/98/99 in Hell. TZ monsters have significantly higher XP and drop rates than regular monsters. In single player, you can terrorize any act using the /terrorize command. TZ areas are shown on the quest log and appear on the automap with a special color.

Heralds are special elite monster packs that spawn in Terror Zones. They are visually distinct (larger, glowing) and always drop at least one item from a special loot table. Heralds spawn randomly within terrorized areas; there is no guaranteed spawn per game — it is entirely RNG-based. Some games you may find 5+ Heralds, others zero. The spawn rate is not tied to Magic Find. Players report that clearing more of the TZ area increases your chances of encountering Heralds, since each monster pack has a small chance to be replaced by a Herald pack. Herald density varies by zone size and monster density.

Sunder Charms are unique grand charms that break a specific immunity on the player (e.g., Cold Sunder breaks Cold Immunity on monsters but also reduces the player's cold resistance by 75). Sunder Charms can ONLY drop from Heralds in Terror Zones — they do not drop from any other source. There are 6 Sunder Charms: The Black Cleft (Physical), The Bone Break (Magic), The Cold Rupture (Cold), The Crack of the Heavens (Lightning), The Flame Rift (Fire), and The Rotting Fissure (Poison). Each Herald kill has a chance to drop a Sunder Charm, but it is not guaranteed. No patch since their introduction in 2.5 has changed sunder charm drop rates — getting several in one game or none for many games is normal RNG variance. Farming Sunders requires repeatedly running TZ areas and killing Heralds.

Tips for farming Heralds efficiently: (1) Run large, high-density TZ areas like Chaos Sanctuary, Worldstone Keep, Flayer Jungle, Arcane Sanctuary — more monster packs = more chances for Herald spawns. (2) Clear the entire TZ area, don't skip packs. (3) In online play, the TZ rotates hourly; plan routes for the current zone. (4) In single player, use /terrorize to pick Act 1-5 and farm your preferred zone repeatedly. (5) Herald spawn rate is pure RNG per pack; some sessions yield many, others yield none. There is no confirmed bug or mechanic that reduces Herald spawns — dry streaks are normal variance. (6) Players/8 (or full online games) do not affect Herald spawn rate, only drop quality. (7) Heralds draw their Ire when they first notice you — you get a chat message about it.

Best locations for farming high runes (Lo, Sur, Ber, Jah, etc.): (1) Travincal (Hell) — Council members have high monster level and always drop from good treasure classes; fastest runs if you can clear quickly. (2) Lower Kurast (LK) super chests — in single player with /players 7-8, the super chests near campfires have fixed drop patterns that can produce HRs; less effective online. (3) Chaos Sanctuary — high monster density + level 85 area; good for combined loot and runes. (4) Cows (Secret Cow Level) — extreme monster density, all dropping from similar treasure classes. (5) Arcane Sanctuary — ghosts/wraiths have reduced drop tables (no armor/weapons), increasing rune drop chance; kill them over platforms so loot doesn't fall into the void. (6) Terror Zones with high density. (7) Flayer Jungle / Bloody Foothills — high density clearing. Key tip: Magic Find does NOT increase rune drops — prioritize kill speed over MF when rune farming. Running /players 3-5 (or full games online) increases the chance of rune drops per kill.
